Jin is sixteen and starts working at her father's restaurant. Her father opened the business at twenty-one with help from his parents. He left Greece as a teenager and worked in restaurants. Now he has his own place. He was proud of it and smiled. He thinks Jin will take over one day. Jin has no interest in running a restaurant. She wanted to do something that felt important and good. Something that made me feel good and gave me purpose. When she worked at the restaurant, she sometimes cried in the bathroom. She felt trapped and wanted to be free. Jin feels overwhelmed and needs help to feel better. She does not want to run a restaurant for her life. During summer breaks, Jin tried to find jobs in high school. She does not want to run the register or wait tables. Her father saw that Jin wanted to live her own life. He understood it was important for her to follow her dreams. He sees himself in her eyes. Jin's mother has different hopes and dreams for him. She had very high standards for herself and others. Jin feels pressure to make her mother proud. This meant joining many activities and keeping good grades in school. When Jin graduated third, her mother felt sad and wanted more. Jin feels she is not enough, even with success. Jin was always a very creative child. She loved reading, writing, and making art. She tried to make art work that her mother liked. Architecture is a good choice and seems nice. It was creative and also good for money. Her mother supports her interest in architecture and design. After a close friend died, Jin thought about being a therapist. She wanted to help people and make them happy. We grow strong human connections with care and understanding. When she talked about social sciences, her mother felt it was bad. She asked why Jin gives up safety and comfort. Jin feels she must make her mother happy after saying no to her father. She applied to a top school and got accepted. She thinks about helping people and wants a good job. A few days before school, Jin changed her study to sociology and psychology. She did not tell her parents right away. She was afraid they would be disappointed. She knew they wanted her to pick a safe way to succeed. They pushed her hard and wanted her to do well. They wanted the best for her, but they hurt her spirit. Jin and other children want to make their parents proud. They wanted their parents to feel their sacrifice was worth it. But what does that really mean. Did they follow their parents' path or make their own. The problem is often harder because of cultural differences. Many immigrant parents come from places with group cultures. These societies focus on community and help each other. Inheriting a family business is what people expect. People do not encourage individual dreams. Their children are in the United States and they have freedom. They want to be themselves but feel pressure to fit in. Jin chose her own path and did not take her father's business. She often worried her parents did not feel appreciated. But this is not true at all. Choosing her dreams shows her gratitude and happiness. She has a choice because they made sacrifices for her. They made it possible for her to define her future. Was she really meant to follow them when they wanted her choices. Jin's parents came to the United States with very little. Her father left Greece when he was a teenager. Her mother grew up poor and worked on farms with her parents. They worked many hours each day for the life they wanted. They wanted Jin to have chances they did not have. They wanted her to choose safe paths and be happy. Jin is a doctor and helps many sick people. Jin is a lawyer and helps people with their problems. Jin is an engineer and works on important projects. They understood these jobs and liked them. These jobs promised success and made people happy. Jin does not know what people do in an office. She never sees creative jobs and does not know them. She only knew about jobs with clear paths and plans. Law is important and helps people live together. Medicine helps people feel better and stay healthy. Engineering is the design and building of machines and structures. Architecture is the art of designing buildings and spaces. Her parents and their friends understood these jobs well. These jobs looked safe and good for people. When Jin started college, she met other students like her. They talked about their families and shared their stories. People feel pressure to succeed in America and other places. They feel guilty about having things their parents did not have. Students feel pressure to do well because of their parents' sacrifices. It can feel hard to breathe sometimes. A friend told Jin her mother said it is not too late. No matter what Jin did, it was never enough. Jin's friend said her father thought tech was ordinary, not medicine. Those words hurt me when I heard them. They made her feel like she disappointed her parents and their work. A friend studied communications, but her father said she would be homeless. He wanted her to be an engineer, a doctor, or a dentist. But she followed her passion instead. Jin moved to Greece to study and learned she could be independent. Jin listened to the stories and felt she was not alone. Many immigrant children face the same struggle. They wanted to honor their parents and remember their hard work. They wanted to follow their dreams and be happy. They wanted to choose paths that gave them joy and safety. Jin lived in a place for students from many countries and America. This experience changed her life in a big way. It changed her job path and helped her grow. She got her master's degree and worked in intercultural education. She travels the world as a coach and consultant. She worked every day and felt good about her life. Not everyone, even her parents, understands her work and life. But Jin did it anyway. She realizes that it has to be enough for her. She does not need to change for other people's ideas. It was not her job to make other people happy. She is here to live her purpose and do what she wants. Jin learns a big lesson from her immigrant parents. Only she can give herself the love and worth she needs. She chose a life that brings her joy. She did not choose therapy, but she helped people understand each other. Jin goes home and helps her father understand English sometimes. She saw that he worked very hard. He sacrificed a lot for his family and friends. He wanted her to succeed very much. She saw that he understood why she needed her own path. He did the same thing before. Her mother was slow to come around. One day, Jin told her mother about her work success. Jin feels happy because those words mean everything to him. Jin spent her life trying to hear them. Jin knows her parents worked hard for her happiness. Shouldn't she decide what happiness looks like for her. They gave up everything and felt very sad. Their language is different and interesting to learn. Their home is a warm and happy place. Their culture is rich and interesting for many people. Everything is for her. She wants choices so she can be happy. She wants to make decisions for herself and be free. Choosing her dreams does not mean she rejects their sacrifices. It was her best way to show gratitude. She uses the freedom they give her. She used the chances they made. She lives the life they dreamed for her. It was not how they thought it would be. Jin does not follow their path. She creates her own path and feels happy. She does it because of all they did for her. Their sacrifices made her choices possible and helped her life. She chooses joy and feels happy. She chooses a purpose for her life. She honors her immigrant identity and her personal dreams. She connects two worlds and helps people understand each other. Jin lives in a world of her parents' traditions and rules. Jin has a world of dreams and passions. She does not have to choose one or the other. She can honor both and feel proud. She can be thankful for their help and follow her own heart. She can be the daughter they raised and the person she wants to be. That was the bridge we saw yesterday. That was the right balance for us. That is the way to go forward.
